name: Kedah (Malaysia)

colors.symbolism: Islam and the agricultural prosperity of the rice fields

description: A red field with a yellow rectangular canton in the upper hoist, charged with a green wreath of paddy stalks surmounted by a crescent moon.
history: The state flag of Kedah, one of Malaysia's northern states. The yellow canton represents the Sultan; the red field represents the people; the wreath of paddy refers to Kedah's role as the rice bowl of Malaysia.

features.symbolism: a yellow canton charged with a green wreath of paddy and crescent — the Sultan's authority and Kedah as the rice bowl of Malaysia
